title: Astronomy for Mortals: Where infinity becomes understandable
author: Wordsus Team
description: A fascinating journey through the cosmos in 365 daily lessons. From the solar system to the far reaches of the observable universe, discover how space-time works, the stars and the great mysteries of astronomy explained in a simple way.

chapters.title: Gas giants: Majestic colossi of the solar system
chapters.description: We explore the immensity of Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus and Neptune. Learn the amazing secrets of its dense atmospheres, rings and its numerous moons.

chapters.title: Constellations: The ancient figures that guide the night
chapters.description: Learn to identify the most famous constellations in the night sky. Discover their beautiful myths and how to use them to orient yourself among the stars.

chapters.title: Fascinating exoplanets: New worlds beyond the sun
chapters.description: We analyze the search for planets outside the solar system. Learn the techniques to discover them and which ones have a great chance of being alive.

chapters.title: Modern telescopes: Giant eyes looking into the sky
chapters.description: We review the operation of the instruments that revolutionized astronomy, from simple optics to enormous space observatories.

chapters.title: Asteroids and meteorites: The wandering rocks of the galaxy
chapters.description: We differentiate these ancient space debris. Understand what happens when they enter Earth's atmosphere and if they pose a real threat.

chapters.title: The Milky Way: A detailed walk through our spiral house
chapters.description: We travel through our gigantic galaxy. We explore its spiral shape, its dense central core and the incredible number of stars it contains.

chapters.title: The light year: Understanding the enormous distances of space
chapters.description: We explain how we measure the immensity of the universe. Discover why when looking at certain distant stars, we are literally peering into the past.

chapters.title: Majestic comets: Ancient snowballs in space
chapters.description: Accompany these frozen nomads in their orbit. Learn about its main components and how they form their spectacular and brilliant tails in the sun.

chapters.title: The Big Bang: The incredible explosion that caused all this
chapters.description: We go back billions of years to narrate the absolute beginning of the universe. We explain how matter, light and time suddenly emerged.

chapters.title: Bright nebulae: Wonderful birthplaces of stars
chapters.description: Delve into immense clouds of cosmic gas and interstellar dust. Contemplate these nurseries where stars are born with beautiful shapes and colors.

chapters.title: Red dwarfs: The most common stars in our galaxy
chapters.description: Get to know the most abundant and long-lived stars in the cosmos. We explain why they are so small, cold and essential for searching for habitable planets.

chapters.title: Dark matter: The great and invisible mystery of the universe
chapters.description: We address one of the greatest enigmas of science. We analyze how we know that invisible matter exists and how it manages to keep galaxies together.

chapters.title: The force of gravity: The glue that holds the cosmos
chapters.description: Discover the main force that shapes the universe. We easily explain how the most massive bodies manage to curve space and time around them.

chapters.title: Supernovas: The most violent explosions in the entire cosmos
chapters.description: Witness the last gasp of massive stars. Learn how these powerful and immense explosions seed space with all the heavy elements.

chapters.title: Andromeda Galaxy: Our immense and distant royal neighbor
chapters.description: We travel to the enormous galaxy closest to our Milky Way. We analyze its colossal great size and its inevitable and incredible future collision with us.

chapters.title: Total eclipses: The perfect dance of shadows in the sky
chapters.description: Understand the mechanics behind these beautiful astronomical events. We teach you why exactly they occur and how you can observe them safely.

chapters.title: Space junk: The worrying artificial terrestrial ring
chapters.description: We examine the thousands of fragments of inactive satellites orbiting the Earth. Learn about the current risks of the artificial ring and the possible cures.

chapters.title: Pulsars and neutron stars: The lighthouses of the universe
chapters.description: Get closer to the densest and most extreme stellar remnants. We explain how they spin vertiginously, emitting constant pulses of radiation like lighthouses.

chapters.title: Wormholes: Theoretical shortcuts through the cosmos
chapters.description: We delve into theoretical physics to talk about the strange bridges in space-time. Find out if it will actually be possible to use them to travel soon.

chapters.title: The dwarf Pluto: The endearing icy rock in the confines
chapters.description: We revisit the rich history of our ancient ninth planet. We discuss why he was reclassified and the amazing recent data we've discovered.

chapters.title: The expanding universe: The mysterious stellar receding
chapters.description: Discover why all the stars in the cosmos move away from each other. We explain the redshift and the enigmatic force known as dark energy.

chapters.title: Dark energy: The invisible accelerator of the vast universe
chapters.description: We delve into the strange constant engine that accelerates cosmic expansion. Learn about the hypotheses of this energy that dominates most of the cosmos.

chapters.title: The Voyager probes: Our furthest human travelers
chapters.description: We celebrate the pioneering mission of great exploration. We talk about his journey through outer planets, his famous golden disk and the final exit from the system.

chapters.title: Giant radio telescopes: Listening to the echoes of the cosmos
chapters.description: We explore how astronomers observe invisible frequencies of light. Understand how these large antennas pick up distant galaxies and radio waves.

chapters.title: The multiverse theory: Are there other parallel worlds?
chapters.description: We address an extremely amazing topic in cosmology. We discussed the incredible theoretical odds that this universe is just one among many others.

chapters.title: The Asteroid Belt: The inner rocky frontier
chapters.description: We explore the region that separates the rocky planets from the gaseous ones. Find out why this ring of debris never formed a complete planet.

chapters.title: James Webb Telescope: A new era of discovery
chapters.description: We analyze the technological marvel that is rewriting astronomy. Learn how its infrared sensors reveal the first galaxies in the cosmos.

chapters.title: Northern Lights: The majestic solar dance on Earth
chapters.description: Understand the science behind the dancing northern lights. We explain how the solar wind interacts with the magnetic field of our blue planet.

chapters.title: Ceres: The closest dwarf planet to our blue home
chapters.description: We travel to the heart of the asteroid belt to meet its king. Discover the mysteries of Ceres, its bright spots and its possible hidden water.

chapters.title: The Oort Cloud: The immense and icy limit of the system
chapters.description: Enter the theoretical and final frontier of the solar system. Get to know the immense bubble of ice and inactive comets that surrounds our mother star.

chapters.title: Luminous quasars: The brightest beacons in the cosmos
chapters.description: Discover active galactic nuclei powered by supermassive black holes. We explain how they manage to emit more light than their entire galaxy.

chapters.title: Spectroscopy: How we read the code of stars
chapters.description: Learn how astronomers discover what distant stars are made of. We explain how breaking down light reveals the chemistry of the entire universe.

chapters.title: Red giants: The inescapable and furious future of the Sun
chapters.description: We analyze the final stage in the life of medium-sized stars. Find out what will happen when our Sun runs out of fuel and expands through the solar system.

chapters.title: Europa and Enceladus: Alien oceans under the ice
chapters.description: We explore the icy moons of Jupiter and Saturn. Discover why its immense underground oceans are ideal places to search for microbial life.

chapters.title: Binary stars: Systems with two suns in the sky
chapters.description: Learn how most of the stars in our galaxy work. We explore the complex gravitational dance between two or more stars tied together.

chapters.title: Cosmic background radiation: The last echo of the Big Bang
chapters.description: Discover the first light emitted in the history of the entire universe. We explain how this weak radiation confirms the explosive origin of the entire cosmos.

chapters.title: The Magellanic Clouds: The neighboring satellite galaxies
chapters.description: We travel to the two small irregular galaxies that orbit the Milky Way. Learn about its beautiful star clusters and how they interact with our house.

chapters.title: White dwarfs: The dead core of ancient stars
chapters.description: We analyze the dense, hot remains of stars similar to the Sun. Learn how this compressed matter shines weakly until slowly extinguishing.

chapters.title: Drake Equation: Estimating Civilizations in the Cosmos
chapters.description: We review the most famous scientific formula about possible intelligent life. Understand its variables and the real probabilities of not being alone today.

chapters.title: Solar wind: The constant storm of our star
chapters.description: Discover the incessant flow of particles that erupt from the Sun. We explain how space weather affects all the planets and satellites in the system.

chapters.title: Globular clusters: The ancient cities of stars
chapters.description: We explore the immense spherical groupings of very old stars. Learn how these giant swarms formed at the beginning of the galaxy.

chapters.title: Magnetars: The most extreme and powerful magnets in the cosmos
chapters.description: Enter the strange world of hyper-magnetic neutron stars. Discover how its ferocious fields manage to deform everything around it.

chapters.title: Titan and its lakes: Saturn's moon similar to Earth
chapters.description: Accompany our visit to the satellite with a dense atmosphere and rivers of liquid methane. We analyze why it is one of the most fascinating worlds in the system.

chapters.title: Orphan Planets: The dark, cold wandering worlds
chapters.description: Meet the lonely planets floating in the interstellar blackness. We explain how they were expelled from their systems and whether they could harbor life.

chapters.title: The Local Group: The small neighborhood of our galaxy
chapters.description: We place the Milky Way in its closest cosmic context. Discover the dwarf and giant galaxies that travel bound by gravity in our region.

chapters.title: The habitable zone: The delicate limit to harbor life
chapters.description: We analyze the perfect distance between a planet and its parent star. Understand why it is crucial to keep water in a liquid state on the surface.

chapters.title: Meteor shower: The luminous tears of comets
chapters.description: We explain why we see dozens of shooting stars at certain times of the year. Learn how Earth crosses ancient interstellar dust trails.

chapters.title: Heat death: The inevitable, icy end of the universe
chapters.description: We explore the ultimate destiny of the cosmos according to thermodynamics. We discuss what will happen when the last star fades away and only pitch blackness remains.

chapters.title: Stellar parallax: The cunning method for measuring the cosmos
chapters.description: Discover how scientists calculate the distance of nearby stars. Learn the simple but brilliant trigonometry that allows you to map the galaxy.

chapters.title: Kuiper Belt: The vast region of frozen worlds
chapters.description: We travel beyond Neptune into the cold realm of the dwarf planets. Learn about the frozen remains of the very early formation of the solar system.

chapters.title: Event Horizon: The Terrifying Edge of the Black Hole
chapters.description: We are approaching the astronomical limit of no return. Understand exactly what happens to time and light just before falling into the great singularity.

chapters.title: Io and extreme volcanism: Jupiter's frozen hell
chapters.description: We observe the most volcanically active moon in the entire solar system. Discover how Jupiter's intense gravity squeezes Io until its rock melts.

chapters.title: Formation of the solar system: From cosmic dust to planets
chapters.description: We go back almost five billion years. We describe the chaotic process that transformed an immense nebula into our radiant star and its worlds.

chapters.title: Superclusters of galaxies: The immense networks of the universe
chapters.description: We expand our vision to contemplate gigantic cosmic structures. We analyze how galaxies form extensive bright threads and large voids.

chapters.title: Great Impact Theory: The violent origin of the Moon
chapters.description: We explain the main hypothesis about the birth of our satellite. Learn the story of Tea, the ancient protoplanet that collided with Earth.

chapters.title: Gravitational lenses: Giant magnifying glasses in the cosmos
chapters.description: Discover how extreme gravity warps light from distant galaxies. Learn how this incredible visual phenomenon allows us to see very hidden stars.

chapters.title: Uranus: The ice giant that turns sideways to the sun
chapters.description: We travel to the seventh planet in the solar system to understand its strange rotation. Learn about its dark rings and why its poles have been light for decades.

chapters.title: Understanding orbits: The spatial constant decay
chapters.description: We explain the physics of planetary movement and artificial satellites. Understand why things in orbit do not float but fall eternally.

chapters.title: Brown dwarfs: The failed stars of the galaxy
chapters.description: We analyze these strange objects that are neither giant planets nor true stars. Find out why they never managed to light an internal nuclear fire.

chapters.title: The Messier Catalog: Classic Guide to the Deep Sky
chapters.description: We review the curious story of the famous comet hunter. Learn why his list of nuisance objects became the ideal guide for astronomers.

chapters.title: Super-Earths: Colossal rocky worlds in the cosmos
chapters.description: Discover a kind of planet that doesn't exist in our stellar neighborhood. We analyze whether these gigantic rocky worlds could be suitable for life.

chapters.title: The great enigma of antimatter in our universe
chapters.description: We explore the substance that annihilates when colliding with ordinary matter. We discuss why the cosmos is not full of it after the big Big Bang.

chapters.title: Exploration of Mars: Searching for traces of past life
chapters.description: Accompany the amazing robotic vehicles on their journey across the red planet. Understand what scientific evidence they are looking for about ancient Martian oceans.

chapters.title: Light pollution: The veil that hides the sky
chapters.description: We discuss how the artificial lighting of our cities robs us of the wonderful cosmic spectacle. Learn how to protect the darkness of the night.

chapters.title: Speed ​​of light: The absolute limit of the universe
chapters.description: We delve into the insurmountable cosmic speed limit. We explain what would physically happen if you tried to travel as fast as a bright photon.

chapters.title: Open star clusters: Brotherhoods in the cosmos
chapters.description: We travel towards beautiful clusters of young stars like the Pleiades. Discover how they are born from the same nebula and why they will end up separating.

chapters.title: The celestial sphere: Mapping the firmament
chapters.description: Learn how astronomers slice up the night sky to find planets. We explain in a simple way how celestial coordinates work today.

chapters.title: White holes: The amazing cosmic fountains
chapters.description: We explore the extreme opposite of black holes. We analyze what the equations postulate about these theoretical places from which only matter can come out.

chapters.title: Phases of Venus: A light cycle behind thick clouds
chapters.description: We explain why the hottest planet of all has phases similar to those of our moon. Learn how this simple discovery changed history.

chapters.title: Planetary transits: Tiny shadows on the Sun
chapters.description: It accompanies Mercury and Venus when they manage to cross in front of the bright solar disk. Understand the great scientific importance of these amazing events.

chapters.title: Elliptical galaxies: The ancient spheres of the universe
chapters.description: We analyze the gigantic galaxies that lost their spiral structure. Find out why they are mainly populated by old, reddish stars.

chapters.title: Interferometry: Telescopes joining their big eyes
chapters.description: Learn about the incredible technique that allows you to connect distant antennas. Thus they manage to create a virtual observatory that is the same size as the entire planet.

chapters.title: Fermi Paradox: Where is intelligent life hiding?
chapters.description: We face the disturbing question about the lack of extraterrestrial contact. We discuss scientific hypotheses that attempt to provide a sensible answer.

chapters.title: Age of the universe: Calculating the birth of the cosmos
chapters.description: We explain in detail how astrophysicists managed to determine that the entire cosmos came into existence exactly fourteen billion years ago.

chapters.title: Zodiac light: The faint glow of planetary dust
chapters.description: We observe the subtle triangular glow that adorns the horizon after sunset. Discover how interplanetary particles reflect the light of our Sun.

chapters.title: Blue giants: Massive stars with ephemeral life
chapters.description: Get to know the hottest and brightest stars in sidereal space. Understand why they consume their fuel very quickly and go towards an epic dark end.

chapters.title: Solar storms: The magnetic danger of the star
chapters.description: We analyze the gigantic explosions on the surface of our Sun. Learn about their true effects on our satellites, electrical networks and life.

chapters.title: Cosmic inflation: The extreme growth of the universe
chapters.description: We delve into the incredible split second after the original big bang. Learn how space itself expanded faster than light itself.

chapters.title: The origin of comets: Two huge frozen deposits
chapters.description: We differentiate the exact origin of periodic comets. Travel again to the two largest sources of these icy and beautiful space relics.

chapters.title: The Great Attractor: The mysterious force that drags us
chapters.description: Discover the invisible gravitational anomaly pulling on the Milky Way and thousands of neighboring galaxies. We analyze what colossal object could be hidden there.

chapters.title: Moon phases in depth: Common myths and their reality
chapters.description: We clarify misconceptions about the dark side of the moon. Understand how basic spatial geometry creates the beautiful cycles we see month after month.

chapters.title: Hot Jupiters: The Colossal Worlds of Hell
chapters.description: We explore gaseous stars that orbit anxiously close to their parent stars. Discover how some manage to withstand incredible temperatures today.

chapters.title: Space Microlensing: Hunting Deeply Hidden Exoplanets
chapters.description: Learn about an ingenious and amazing method to find strange worlds and nomadic stars. We explain how small stars act as quick visual magnifying glasses.

chapters.title: Multiple star systems: The dance with multiple suns
chapters.description: We go beyond classical binary systems to look at complex groups. We analyze the unstable and very fragile physical mechanics of these orbits.

chapters.title: Lactomeda: The colossal merger of two majestic galaxies
chapters.description: We project billions of years into the future. We analyze step by step the great imminent collision between our galaxy and the great Andromeda.

chapters.title: Saturn's rings: A system of icy jewels
chapters.description: Discover what the famous rings of the sixth planet are made of. We explain how they formed and why they might disappear in the distant future.

chapters.title: Gravitational waves: Listening to the wrinkles of the cosmos
chapters.description: We delve into the vibrations of space-time. Learn how the collisions of immense black holes allow us to perceive the invisible universe.

chapters.title: Asterisms: The unofficial figures of the night sky
chapters.description: We differentiate these figures from the classic constellations. Learn to find the Great Chariot or the Summer Triangle on your observing nights.

chapters.title: Mount Olympus: The immense volcano on the planet Mars
chapters.description: We visited the largest mountain in our entire solar system. We analyze why Mars could form a colossal volcano that dwarfs those on Earth.

chapters.title: The Roche limit: The frontier of space destruction
chapters.description: We explain the invisible boundary around giant planets. Find out why moons that get too close end up shattered by gravity.

chapters.title: Cosmic rays: High-energy particles from space
chapters.description: We analyze the fast microscopic projectiles that bombard the Earth. Learn about its mysterious origin and how our atmosphere manages to protect us today.

chapters.title: The cosmic albedo: The reflective brilliance of the worlds
chapters.description: Understand why some planets shine brighter than others in the sky. We explain how ice or rock surfaces reflect the intense light of the Sun.

chapters.title: Oumuamua: The first and strange interstellar visitor
chapters.description: We review the mystery of the cigar-shaped object that crossed our system. We analyze the theories about its true origin in another star.

chapters.title: The Hubble sequence: Classifying all the galaxies
chapters.description: Learn about the famous tuning fork diagram created to organize the cosmos. Learn to visually distinguish between spiral and elliptical shapes.

chapters.title: Neutrinos: Trapping cosmic ghost particles
chapters.description: Discover how astronomers observe particles passing through matter. Understand what they reveal to us about the core of the Sun and distant supernovae.

chapters.title: Fireballs: The brightest meteors in the sky
chapters.description: We differentiate common shooting stars from these larger rocks. We explain why they generate powerful flashes and explosions when they touch the atmosphere.

chapters.title: Tidal coupling: The anchoring of our Moon
chapters.description: We explain why we always see the same face of our natural satellite. Learn how Earth's immense gravity slowed its rotation centuries ago.

chapters.title: The Local Bubble: The immense void where the Sun travels
chapters.description: We explore the cavity of hot gas surrounding our stellar neighborhood. Learn how ancient supernovae cleared this large area of ​​the Milky Way.

chapters.title: X-ray astronomy: Observing the violent universe
chapters.description: Discover what we see when we look at the most energetic frequencies. Learn how this light reveals super-hot gas around fierce black holes.

chapters.title: Spaghettization: The strange final fate of falling
chapters.description: Delve into the theoretical physics of black holes. Understand step by step why the enormous gravitational difference would stretch any physical object.

chapters.title: Triton: Neptune's large, rebellious, frozen moon
chapters.description: We visit the enormous satellite that orbits in the opposite direction to its planet. We analyze its strange nitrogen geysers and their possible distant origin.

chapters.title: Tilt axis: The true engine of the stations
chapters.description: We easily explain why we experience winter and summer. Discover how Earth's slight tilt dramatically changes our global climate.

chapters.title: Dyson spheres: Megastructures for stars
chapters.description: We discuss a popular theoretical concept for advanced civilizations. We analyze how they could surround their stars to capture all their immense energy.

chapters.title: The barycenter: The balance of stellar orbits
chapters.description: Discover that planets do not actually orbit the exact center of the Sun. Understand this incredible gravitational balance point in systems.

chapters.title: Proxima Centauri: Visiting our neighboring star
chapters.description: We imaginarily travel to the closest star system to Earth. Get to know this red dwarf and the rocky planets orbiting it today.

chapters.title: Spatial curvature: The amazing fabric of the universe
chapters.description: We delve into modern gravity explained by the great Albert Einstein. Imagine how the mass deforms space, creating invisible dips.

chapters.title: Time dilation: When the clock ticks differently
chapters.description: Understand why time does not flow the same for all observers. We explain how immense speed or intense gravity alters your own clock.

chapters.title: Ceres and Vesta: The kings of the great rocky belt
chapters.description: We return to the minor worlds to compare these two fascinating bodies. Discover the great differences in their composition, geology and final shape.

chapters.title: The Great Red Spot: Jupiter's eternal storm
chapters.description: We observe the gigantic and ferocious hurricane that has been active for centuries. We analyze why its winds do not stop and how its enormous size is reduced today.

chapters.title: The long journey of the photon: From the nucleus to you
chapters.description: Follow the epic journey of a single particle of light. Learn how it takes thousands of years for it to leave the Sun to cross space and reach your own eyes.

chapters.title: The Kardashev Scale: Classifying aliens
chapters.description: We explore the theoretical method to measure the advancement of possible civilizations. We analyze its three types based on the enormous amount of energy used.

chapters.title: Eclipsing binaries: The stars that hide
chapters.description: Learn about double systems that vary their brightness regularly. We explain how a star blocks the light of its companion from our perspective.

chapters.title: Galactic halos: The vast sphere of dark matter
chapters.description: We expand our vision of galaxies. Discover the colossal invisible structure that surrounds the spiral discs and holds them perfectly together.

chapters.title: The Boötes Void: The loneliest area in the cosmos
chapters.description: We travel to an immense region containing astonishingly few galaxies. We discuss how these gigantic bubbles of the cosmic web formed.

chapters.title: The heliopause: The distant and turbulent solar limit
chapters.description: We reach the exact border where the solar wind loses its immense force. Know the precise point where interstellar space officially begins.

chapters.title: Sagittarius A star: The central monster of the galaxy
chapters.description: We explore the immense supermassive black hole that lives in the heart of the Milky Way. Discover how astronomers managed to map its powerful environment.

chapters.title: Lunar eclipses: The mystery of the blood moon
chapters.description: Understand why our satellite occasionally turns intense red. We explain the exact alignment and the role of the atmosphere in this great phenomenon.

chapters.title: The TRAPPIST-1 system: Seven very promising worlds
chapters.description: We travel to the famous dwarf star that houses several Earth-sized planets. We discussed their close orbits and the real possibilities of having life.

chapters.title: Kilonovas: The explosive collision of heavy stars
chapters.description: Learn what happens when two neutron stars violently collide. Discover how these events forge precious metals like gold on our planet.

chapters.title: Solar cycles: The magnetic beats of our star
chapters.description: We analyze the periodic changes in the activity of our Sun. Learn how the rise and fall of sunspots affects the climate of the entire system.

chapters.title: Galactic cannibalism: When galaxies merge
chapters.description: We observe how large galaxies grow by devouring their smaller neighbors. Understand the gravitational forces that destroy assimilating countless stars today.

chapters.title: Lagrange Points: The cosmic parking areas
chapters.description: We explain the spatial areas where the gravity of two large bodies balances. Learn why we place our key satellites and telescopes there.

chapters.title: The Betelgeuse case: An imminent stellar explosion
chapters.description: We analyze the strange dimming of this red supergiant of Orion. We discuss whether we are about to witness its spectacular death as a large supernova.

chapters.title: Exoplanet Hunters: Kepler and TESS Missions Today
chapters.description: We celebrate the impressive telescopes designed to search for new worlds. Discover the ingenious techniques that have revealed thousands of planetary systems.

chapters.title: Cosmic strings: The fissures of the early universe
chapters.description: We delve into theoretical cosmology to study these spatial rifts. Learn how microscopic defects in a vacuum harbor unimaginable energy.

chapters.description: Learn about the active galaxies that point their energy jets towards Earth. We explain how powerful black holes shoot these beams of radiation.

chapters.title: The ninth planet: In search of the hidden giant
chapters.description: We explore intriguing gravitational clues in the far reaches of our system. We discussed the strong possibility of having a huge dark planet to discover.

chapters.title: Valles Marineris: The enormous great scar on Mars today
chapters.description: We visit the largest and most spectacular canyon system in the solar system. We analyze the mysterious tectonic processes that managed to tear the planet apart.

chapters.title: Eyeball Planets: Worlds of Fire and Eternal Ice
chapters.description: Discover peculiar exoplanets anchored to their star by strong gravity. We explain how their extreme and opposite faces generate climates of astonishing contrast.

chapters.title: The theory of panspermia: Space seeds of life
chapters.description: We address the fascinating idea that life did not originate on Earth. We discussed how comets could transport microorganisms traveling through the cosmos.

chapters.title: The star diagram: Charting the lives of stars
chapters.description: Learn about the main visual tool that radically changed astrophysics. Learn to interpret the complete life cycle of a star by measuring light and heat.

chapters.title: Impact Craters: The Earth's rocky scars
chapters.description: We look at how brutal collisions of large asteroids shaped our world. We review the visible traces and their great impact on planetary life.

chapters.description: We travel to the first exoplanets that were discovered in history. Be surprised by dead stars that rotate emitting very high doses of energy.

chapters.description: We pay tribute to the iconic observatory that revolutionized human knowledge. We review his great visual discoveries and how he transformed current science.

chapters.title: Fascinating exomoons: Satellites beyond the system
chapters.description: We expand the tireless astronomical search towards possible distant moons. We discuss whether these extrasolar satellites could host a new biosphere.

chapters.title: Dark Flow: The Strange Galactic Co-Motion
chapters.description: We explore the mysterious mass movement of galactic clusters together. We analyze what invisible external force could constantly attract them.

chapters.title: The Pleiades: The beautiful and brilliant flock of stars

chapters.title: Pillars of Creation: A majestic stellar nursery

chapters.title: The geysers of Enceladus: Shooting ice into the cosmos

chapters.title: The great filter: The difficult survival in the galaxy

chapters.title: The great singularity: The heart of the black hole

chapters.description: We descend into the thin visible layer surrounding our fiery parent star. Understand why it turns out to be the only solar boundary we observe.

chapters.title: The Martian polar corona: The ice of the red planet
chapters.description: We analyze the large caps of water and frozen carbon dioxide that crown the planet Mars. Discover how they change with their extreme seasons.

chapters.title: Galactic rotation curves: The total stellar spin
chapters.description: We explain the ancient mystery of the rapid spiral motion of giant galaxies. Understand how this phenomenon revealed the great dark matter.

chapters.title: The LISA space telescope: Hunting for hidden waves
chapters.description: We look toward the great future of exploration with this gigantic orbital observatory designed to measure the immense wrinkles of spacetime today.

chapters.title: Brown subdwarfs: Dark worlds floating in isolation
chapters.description: Meet the strange solitary stars that are smaller than typical failed stars but share the great void far from any burning sun.

chapters.title: Solar chromosphere: The thin, reddish layer of fire
chapters.description: We explore the unstable middle atmosphere of our fiery daytime star. Observe its towering and immense jets of bright and untamable plasma.

chapters.title: Core accretion: The origin of vast worlds
chapters.description: We detail the main theory about the origin of the Jovian planets. Learn how the small, solid, rocky core attracted dense and abundant gases.

chapters.title: Solar Sails: Navigating Space with Pure Light
chapters.description: Discover the fascinating and innovative technology that allows modern probes to travel immense distances using only the impulse of fast photons.

chapters.title: Microquasars: The Fierce Fast Galactic Engines
chapters.description: We explore the incredible smaller-scale replicas of large quasars. Understand how these dark binaries emit jets of immense life energy.

chapters.title: The Stochastic Background of Cosmic Gravitational Waves
chapters.description: Immerse yourself in the constant hum produced by the entire universe in the background. We analyze how the echo of countless titanic clashes reverberates in our time.

chapters.title: The Chicxulub crater: The impact that changed everything today
chapters.description: We visit the enormous terrestrial scar left by the great devastating asteroid that erased the imposing dinosaurs, changing our life history today.

chapters.title: The limit of the observable universe: The edge of sight
chapters.description: We reach the maximum frontier of our human cosmological vision. Understand why it is absolutely impossible to see beyond this spherical horizon.

chapters.title: Bok Globules: The dense cold stellar incubators
chapters.description: Delve into the dark, dense clouds of cosmic dust and gas. Discover how these tiny but heavy cocoons manage to gestate new stars.

chapters.title: Protoplanetary disks: Forging brilliant worlds today
chapters.description: We observe the immense swirls of gas and rocks that surround the infant suns. We explain the chaotic process of building a planetary system.

chapters.title: The Pioneer Anomaly: The great mystery of gravity
chapters.description: We analyze the strange force that slowed down our first distant space probes. Understand how the simple heat emitted managed to alter its trajectory.

chapters.title: Rare Earth Hypothesis: Our fragile blue oasis
chapters.description: We discuss the fascinating theory that suggests that microbial life is common, but complex, intelligent life is extremely rare in the cosmos.

chapters.title: Sunspots: The cold, dark magnetic islands
chapters.description: We explore the dark regions that appear above the bright solar photosphere. Discover how these intense magnetic knots regulate space weather.

chapters.title: The immense Virgo Cluster: Our galactic neighborhood
chapters.description: We travel to the gravitational center of our local supercluster. Learn about the massive grouping of thousands of galaxies that dominate this large region of the universe.

chapters.title: Black dwarfs: The icy, dark cosmic ashes
chapters.description: Enter the farthest and darkest future of the universe. We explain how white dwarfs will cool completely until they become undetectable rocks.

chapters.title: Laws of Johannes Kepler: The beautiful dance of the planets
chapters.description: We review the elegant mathematical rules that describe planetary orbits. Learn why worlds accelerate as they approach their parent star.

chapters.title: Shoemaker-Levy Comet: The Incredible Fast Jovian Impact
chapters.description: We relive the historic event of nineteen ninety-four. We look at how Jupiter captured and destroyed a comet, receiving a massive bombardment.

chapters.title: Lunar occultations: Shadows on distant stars
chapters.description: Learn about events where our satellite blocks the light of distant stars. Understand how this helps accurately measure stellar dimensions.

chapters.title: The Hubble Tension: The dilemma of the expansion of the cosmos
chapters.description: We address the greatest crisis of modern cosmology. We explain why the different measurements of the expansion of the universe fail to coincide today.

chapters.title: Galactic winds: The fierce storms of dark gas
chapters.description: We observe the massive streams of particles ejected by the active nuclei. Discover how they manage to slow or stop the formation of new stars.

chapters.title: Martian storms: The fine dust that covers the entire world
chapters.description: We visit the red planet to analyze its ferocious global hurricanes. Understand how its atmosphere manages to raise dust, obscuring its distant sun.

chapters.title: Van Allen Belts: The immense blue magnetic trap
chapters.description: We explore the rings of intense radiation that surround the Earth. We explain how they manage to trap fast solar particles, protecting our lives.

chapters.title: Amorphous Space Ice: Today's Dark Crystalless Sea
chapters.description: Discover the most common state of water throughout our vast, cold universe. We look at this strange, shapeless ice that sticks to stardust.

chapters.title: The radiative zone of the Sun: The very slow journey of pure light
chapters.description: Delve into the dense middle layer of our parent star. Understand why a single photon can take millennia to traverse this great labyrinth.

chapters.title: Titania: The immense and very scarred moon of the great Uranus
chapters.description: We visit the largest Uranian satellite. We discussed its gigantic canyons and enormous geological faults that evidence a violent and frozen distant past.

chapters.title: The oscillating universe: From the Big Bang to the big bounce
chapters.description: We immerse ourselves in the hypotheses about the eternal cycle of the infinite cosmos. We discuss whether our universe will contract again to be born again.

chapters.title: The Nancy Roman Telescope: Expanding the view of the cosmos
chapters.description: Learn about the future of space infrared exploration. We analyze how this great mission will seek to understand dark energy by mapping the entire universe.

chapters.title: Dark ice in the deep craters of our Moon
chapters.description: We explore the corners of the lunar poles that never receive direct sunlight. Discover why water reserves are key to the human future.

chapters.title: Stellar spectroscopy: The incredible and vital chemical fingerprint
chapters.description: Understand how stellar atmospheres devour specific colors of light. We explain how these dark lines reveal the exact elements of the star.

chapters.title: Teegarden Star: A very close and former neighbor
chapters.description: We traveled to this exceptionally close and old little red dwarf. We analyze its two rocky exoplanets that could have large crystalline seas.

chapters.title: Large radio galaxies: The distant emitters in the vacuum
chapters.description: We observe the most extensive and energetic structures of the immense cosmos. Learn how invisible plasma jets emit powerful millimeter waves.

chapters.title: Differential rotation: The strange turn of the giants
chapters.description: Discover why the huge gaseous planets do not rotate like solid bodies. We explain how its equator travels much faster than its cold poles.

chapters.title: The rain of diamonds deep in the frost giants
chapters.description: Delve into the immense pressures of Uranus and Neptune. We analyze the chemical process where methane is compressed forming rich and precious solid jewels.

chapters.title: Jupiter's immense and very powerful magnetic field today
chapters.description: We explore the enormous Jovian magnetosphere. Learn how its immense size manages to trap lethal radiation, creating eternal auroras a thousand times brighter.

chapters.title: Neutrino oscillation: Particles that manage to mutate today
chapters.description: We address the great mystery of solar particles. We explain how neutrinos manage to mysteriously alter their own mass while crossing the vacuum.

chapters.title: Eridanus Supervoid: The great and only cosmic cold zone
chapters.description: We travel towards the most disconcerting empty structure in the celestial firmament. We discussed what drove the matter away, leaving this big, dark, cold hole.

chapters.title: Rings of debris and dust on very distant asteroids
chapters.description: Break the myth that only big worlds have big rings. Discover how smaller rocks like Chariclo manage to maintain their own ring of debris.

chapters.title: Cosmic thermal evolution: The slow universal cooling
chapters.description: We review the immense history of the temperature of the great known universe. Understand how we went from a super dense hell to the almost absolute cold of today.

chapters.title: Variable stars Look: The rhythmic stellar beats
chapters.description: We observe red giants that change their enormous brightness in just a few months. We analyze what causes this great expansion and contraction of its final layers.

chapters.title: Apollo Reflectors: Measuring the Moon's Distance Today
chapters.description: Learn about the amazing laser experiment that is still in force. Understand how we verify with millimeter precision that our beautiful moon moves away every year.

chapters.title: The Great Expanding Cosmic Web: Dark Galactic Threads
chapters.description: Contemplate the most immense and grandiose structure in the visible and known cosmos. We explain how the enigmatic dark matter manages to weave the modern universe.

chapters.title: Black hole thermodynamics: Hawking Radiation today
chapters.description: We immerse ourselves in the complex theories of the enormous physicist Stephen Hawking. Discover why even the most voracious devourers of light manage to evaporate today.

chapters.title: Cosmic Chronology: The Inexorable Journey of Time Today
chapters.description: We explore the temporal arrow of our immense universe. Understand how inexorable entropy ensures that the natural flow of events never reverses.
